title: Conway 2D Moore-9 shared-P translation-invariant baseline — Law 64 v6 critical falsifier

t8l_anchor.interpretation: shared-P-9 closes 166/169 of per-cell-o3 gap at SAME data volume; full closure at N>=200

law_64_v6_status: RECONFIRMED — proper matched-neighborhood translation-invariant baseline saturates Conway given >=20k samples (N>=200, ~40 samples/context). v7 NOT required.
interpretation: Critical falsifier PASSED. T8l per-cell baselines fundamentally cannot represent Conway's 2D Moore-9 update rule (1D contexts, no spatial structure). When a translation-invariant matched-neighborhood baseline is used, advantage collapses from +169 (per-cell o3) to +3 at N=50 and zero at N>=200. The 'CA(5) advantage' in T8l reflected baseline mis-specification, NOT a structural CA advantage. Law 64 v6 (CA window >= substrate neighborhood => perfect prediction) holds robustly for Conway when the baseline is honestly dimensioned.

raw_91.C1_falsifiable: YES — thresholds 5/50 per_thousand at N=200/N=5000 frozen pre-run
raw_91.C2_replicable: YES — seed 20260428 deterministic, RNG reset per N
raw_91.C3_no_p_hacking: YES — N_STEPS values [50,200,1000,5000] and verdict mapping frozen in tool header before run
raw_91.C4_data_caveats: 10x10 / 32% random density / toroidal Moore-9 — NOT glider-specific; saturation depends on context coverage (481/512 at N>=200)
raw_91.C5_negative_result_published: YES — verdict emitted regardless of outcome

uncovered_contexts_at_n5000: 31/512 contexts never observed even at 250000 cell-positions training — these correspond to Moore-9 patterns the orbit never reaches from this initial condition; they default to p=500 (predict 0). Test set apparently never queries them either — perfect accuracy holds.
